summary.brief: Montgomery v. Gardiner. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: Dr. Jeffrey P. Gardiner, an ophthalmologist employed by Utah Valley Eye Center, Inc., performed cataract surgery on Amber Montgomery’s left eye.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: After the surgery, Montgomery experienced poor eyesight and other increasingly troubling vision problems in that eye.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Nearly three months after the surgery, an on-call ophthalmologist diagnosed Montgomery with multiple retinal tears and retinal detachment.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Montgomery has not regained vision in her left eye.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Montgomery sued Gardiner and Utah Valley Eye Center, alleging that Gardiner’s delay in diagnosing her retinal detachment caused her vision loss.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Expert was asked to review Montgomery’s imaging from October 12. He pointed out markers that '[i]n most cases . . . would indicate a retinal detachment.'
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: He reaffirmed that 'an urgent diagnosis and treatment referral is the standard of care associated with the ophthalmological practice of diagnosing retinal tears and treating them.'
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: The court agreed and granted the motion.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: We conclude that the expert was qualified to testify as to causation and that Montgomery did put on evidence of causation from which a jury could find in her favor.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: We therefore reverse the district court’s judgment as a matter of law and remand this matter for retrial or other appropriate proceedings consistent with this opinion.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Blurry vision and discomfort in left eye post-cataract surgery
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient underwent cataract surgery on left eye on September 18, 2017. Post-surgery, experienced blurry vision, discomfort, flashes of light, and floaters.
past_medical_history: Previous cataract surgery on right eye

focused_exam.eye: Blurry vision, discomfort, flashes of light, floaters in left eye

requestables.name: Retinal scan
requestables.description: Scan of the retina to check for detachment

reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates need for urgent referral to retina specialist
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt immediate referral for surgical intervention

timeline_phases.name: Initial Post-operative Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with symptoms following cataract surgery

key_events.event: First post-operative visit with blurry vision reported

timeline_phases.name: Follow-up and Diagnosis
timeline_phases.description: Patient undergoes follow-up visits and eventual diagnosis

key_events.event: Retinal detachment diagnosed by on-call ophthalmologist

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with post-operative blurry vision and discomfort
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.clinical_reasoning: Urgent referral needed to prevent progression of retinal detachment

options.description: Advise increased water intake and prescribe new contact lenses
options.clinical_reasoning: Inadequate response to symptoms indicating possible retinal detachment

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant advised increased water intake and prescribed new contact lenses
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant believed symptoms were due to dry eyes

expected_action_court.description: Immediate referral to retina specialist should have been made
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established urgent referral is standard

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to recognize and act on signs of retinal detachment
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Immediate referral to prevent further vision loss
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient suffered permanent vision loss in left eye
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Loss of vision in left eye

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to urgently diagnose and treat retinal detachment
Causation established - delay contributed to vision loss

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to urgently diagnose and treat retinal detachment
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Urgent intervention could have preserved vision

factual_timeline.timestamp: October 12, Date not specified in judgment

tests_performed.date: October 12, Date not specified in judgment

diagnoses.when_made: Nearly three months after initial presentation

treatments.outcome: Retina reattached but vision not restored

allegations.allegation: Failure to urgently diagnose and treat retinal detachment

court_findings.legal_significance: Judgment as a matter of law granted to defendant

court_findings.finding: Breach of duty in failing to urgently diagnose and treat retinal detachment
